titleStmt.title: SII 16.110: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 110. (A.R. No. 492 of 1906.) PULIVENDLA, PULIVENDLA TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On two faces of a slab lying at the entrance of the Āñjanēyasvāmin temple.
This is dated Śaka 1457, Manmatha, Bhādrapada, śu. 1, corresponding to 1535 A.D., August 29, (Sunday).

The inscription is in the form of a charter (nammika śāsanam) granted to the merchants, reḍḍis, karaṇams and others by Tuḷuva Yallappa-nāyaniṁgāru, the agent of Tiṁmarāju Salakayyadēva-mahārāju, in order to rehabilitate the village, revising the taxes and levying a maximum fine of 12 rūkas in case of default.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīgaṇādhipatayē namaḥ [.]
śubhamastu . svastiśrī jayābhyu
daya śālivāhana śakavaruṣa
ṁbulu 1457 Agunēṭi ma
nmatha saṁvatsara bhādrapada śu 1
śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrī vī
rapratāpa śrī Acyutadēvamahārāya
lu pṛthivirājyaṁ cēyucuṁḍaṁgā
nu tiṁmarāju salakayyadēvamahā
rājula Ayyavāri kā(¨r)ryaka¨rttalai
na tuluva yallappa nāyaniṁgāru pu
liviṁdala sīmanu vunna seṭṭipaṭṇasvāmi
reḍlu karaṇālu Aṣṭādaśaprajalu mōda
laina kāpulaku yiccina nammika śāsana krama
meṭlannanu vūru viḍicipōyina kāpula
ku kottagānu vaccē kāṁpulaku vūranu vu
ṁnna kāṁpulaku nēmu yevvariki yē mari
yādaku taṁkaṭumaṭlu sēstimi Ā mariyā
danē yiccina māṭapaṭṭu tappaka naḍapaṁgala
vāraṁ nēmu yiccina cīṭipramāṇaṁ
pannupeṭuka grāmāna sukhāna vuṁḍē
di pū¨rvvāna Accē pannu peṭṭalēka pōyina

kāpula kāpulaku pū
¨rvapannu māni nēmu kaṭṭumaṭṭu cēsi
na pannaku ku yēmi nēraṁ sēsinā
Aparādhaṁ paṁḍreṁḍu rūkalēkāni mari
yekuḍu konarādu yī śāsanamulōna
vuṁnna mariyādaku puliviṁdalaśīma yē
lē doralu yevvaru tappikonnānu gaṁgakaru
tanu gōvu brāṁhmaṇavadha sēsina dōṣāna
bōvuvāru tama tallitaṁḍṟla vānakāsi
lō vadhiṁccina dōṣāna bōvuvāru yiṁdu
ku yē doralu tappikonnānu yallappanā
yini Ayyavāri guraṁpaṭṭē vāniki ta
na peṁḍlāmunu yiccinavāṁḍu .
Digital edition of SII 16.110 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
